title: Hydraulic whipstock anchor
abstract: A whipstock anchor is hydraulically set and locked in the set position. Release occurs with a pull induced component failure that relieves hydraulic pressure that allows the slips to retract. Release can occur with a remotely actuated circuit that burns a retainer for a piston whose movement opens a vent or initiates a chemical reaction to undermine a lock ring. Movement of a single cone or opposed cones extends the slips. The cone angles being different (cone angles do not have to be different, it is preferred to have the slip angles different) adds a skew to the slips and positions the top of the whipstock against the tubular top in a horizontal run. A bottom cap is removable to convert to setting by set down weight or to attach a hydraulically operated packer below the slips. Slips can be extended with radial movement of pistons.
claims: Claims We claim: 1. An anchored borehole tool assembly, comprising: a borehole tool (10,12); an anchor housing (22,24,26) featuring at least one radially extendible slip (36) actuated by at least one piston (32) selectively operated by hydraulic pressure (14,16,18) provided into said housing (20); characterized by said housing further comprising a pressure retaining device (16) to hold said at least one slip (36) extended by holding hydraulic pressure applied to said housing (20,32,34). 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said hydraulic pressure in said housing is relieved with failure of a component (60) in said housing allowing said slip (36) to be retracted. 3. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said hydraulic pressure in said housing is relieved with opening a vent valve (86,88,96) in said housing allowing said slip (36) to be retracted. 4. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said hydraulic pressure in said housing is relieved with undermining (86,88,96 130,90) at least one locking member (44) for said at least one slip (36) in said housing allowing said slip to be retracted. 5. The assembly of claim 4, wherein: said at least one locking member is undermined by opening a valve (86,88,96) to release a fluid to reach said at least one locking member(42). 6. The assembly of claim 5, wherein: said valve (86,88,96) is signaled to open from a remote location without borehole intervention. 7. The assembly of claim 6, wherein: said valve comprises a selectively retained piston (86), whereupon said remote signal a restraint on said piston (86) is removed such that piston movement opens a fluid reservoir (130) to said at least one locking member (42) to undermine said at least one locking member with said fluid. 8. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said at least one piston is selectively mechanically operated for extension of said at least one slip by exposing said at least one piston (32) with removal (24) of a cover (22) on said housing. 9. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: removal of a cover (22) on said housing adapts said housing to accept a packer assembly (126) for hydraulic operation using the hydraulic pressure in said housing that operates said at least one slip (36). 10. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: a mandrel (58) is surrounded by said at least one piston (32); movement of said piston (32) relative to said mandrel is locked with a locking member (44) against reverse movement of said at least one piston; said mandrel having a decreased dimension portion (60) that breaks or fails in response to a tensile force on said tool transmitted to one end of said mandrel with an opposing end of said mandrel retained by said at least one slip (36) resisting said tensile force through said at least one piston and said locking member (44); said breaking or failing of said decreased dimension portion (60) of said mandrel allows a portion of said mandrel to move away from said at least one slip (36) for retraction of said at least one slip. 11. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said at least one slip comprises a plurality of pistons (32,58) disposed for movement in opposite directions toward said at least one slip (36) with each piston locked (44,70) against reverse movement away from said at least one slip. 12. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said tool has a first longitudinal axis (152) and said slips have a second longitudinal axis that is skewed with respect to said first longitudinal axis such that extension of said at least one slip cocks an end of said tool toward a wall of the borehole. 13. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said at least one slip comprises a plurality of circumferentially spaced slips (36) each of said slips having opposed end tapers (48,50) that slide on opposing ramps, wherein said tapers have the same angle with said opposing ramps at different angles or said tapers (48,50) have the different angles with said opposing tapers having the same angle such that extension of said slips cocks an end of said tool toward a wall of the borehole. 14. An anchored borehole tool assembly, comprising: a borehole tool (10, 12); an anchor housing featuring a plurality of radially extendible slips (36) actuated by at least one piston (32) selectively operated by hydraulic pressure provided into said housing(14,16, 18); characterized by wherein radial extension of said slips (36) skews the axis (152) of at least said borehole tool with respect to an axis of the borehole. 15. The assembly of claim 13, wherein: said slips (36) having different widths on opposed sides of a retainer to insure the proper installation orientation. 16. The assembly of claim 14, wherein: a mandrel (58) is surrounded by said at least one piston; movement of said piston relative to said mandrel is locked with a locking member (44) against reverse movement of said at least one piston; said mandrel having a decreased dimension portion (60) that breaks or fails in response to a tensile force on said tool transmitted to one end of said mandrel with an opposing end of said mandrel retained by said slips resisting said tensile force through said at least one piston (36) and said locking member (44); said breaking or failing of said decreased dimension portion of said mandrel allows a portion of said mandrel (58) to move away from said slips for retraction of said slips. 17. The assembly of claim 14, wherein: said tool has a first longitudinal axis and said slips have a second longitudinal axis that is skewed with respect to said first longitudinal axis such that extension of said slips cocks an end of said tool toward a wall of the borehole. 18. The assembly of claim 14, wherein: said slips are circumferentially spaced such that each of said slips comprises opposed end tapers that slide on opposing ramps, wherein said tapers have the same angle with said opposing ramps (48,50) at different angles or said tapers have the different angles with said opposing tapers having the same angle such that extension of said slips cocks an end of said tool toward a wall of the borehole.
